recording_notes: DIRECT CAMERA RECORDING — camera inside blue FLS trainer box looking directly at task area. Blue plastic trainer with red clamps, duct tape covering, pink/red foam, dark suture. BEST RECORDING QUALITY since V15. Similar setup to V15 (STORZ-style instruments, direct view). NOT the V28-V29 station. 1280x720 at 30fps. Instruments already partially visible at frame 1.

frame_analyses.description: DIRECT CAMERA VIEW inside FLS trainer. Blue box with red clamps, duct tape, pink foam. Drain with Penrose visible. One instrument from left with dark suture and needle. TIMING STARTS ~1-2s.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Best recording quality since V15. Dark suture clearly visible. Excellent detail.

frame_analyses.description: Both instruments at drain. Right instrument driving needle through drain. Left instrument stabilizing. Dark suture clearly visible extending from needle. Active placement.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Placement in progress. Good bimanual technique visible in direct view.

frame_analyses.description: Suture placed. Both instruments at drain with suture ends visible. First throw wrapping beginning. Dark suture extending from drain to instrument.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Placement complete ~28s (~26s from first instrument). Starting first throw.

frame_analyses.description: First throw wrapping/tightening. Both instruments spread wide above drain. Dark suture arcing high between instruments. Classic V-spread tightening pattern.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First throw in progress. Clear suture arc visible — excellent technique visualization.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw in progress. Instruments at drain. Suture crossing at drain center. Knot building.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First throw complete ~58s (~30s). Second throw wrapping.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw wrapping. Both instruments at drain. Multiple dark suture crossings visible at knot. Drain starting to compress.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Second throw complete ~72s (~14s). Third throw in progress. Good pace.

frame_analyses.description: Knot complete. Suture tails visible extending from knot — one to upper right, one extending down. Instrument at drain positioning for cutting. Dark suture clearly visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Third throw complete ~90s (~18s). Throws total ~62s. Cutting starting.

frame_analyses.description: Cutting in progress. Both instruments above drain. Suture tail arcing upward held by right instrument while left positions scissors. Knot visible at drain center.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: ~33s into cutting. Working on second tail.

frame_analyses.description: Final cutting. One instrument at drain with short remaining tail. Knot visible — compact with dark suture wrapping. Nearly complete.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: ~63s of cutting. Still the bottleneck phase. Nearing completion.

frame_analyses.description: Task complete. Instruments withdrawn. EXCELLENT FINAL VIEW: Compact knot at drain center with very short dark tails. Drain slit appears WELL CLOSED — edges approximated tightly around knot. One suture tail extends slightly to left onto duct tape. Drain not avulsed. Clean, professional result.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Completion ~160s. Total from first instrument: ~158s. HIGHEST CONFIDENCE final assessment in series due to direct camera.

knot_assessments.notes: First throw 28-58s (30s). Surgeon's knot — double wrap visible in direct camera. V-spread tightening at 50s. Good technique. Confidence: HIGH.
Second throw 58-72s (14s). Fast, efficient single throw. Hand switch confirmed — left instrument leads. Confidence: MODERATE.
Third throw 72-90s (18s). Hand switch back confirmed. Drain compression visible. Confidence: MODERATE.

suture_placement.placement_notes: Direct camera allows better assessment. Needle pass appears through or very near both marks. Marks partially visible on gauze. More accurate than any monitor-based estimate.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: HIGHEST CONFIDENCE drain assessment in series. At 165s, direct camera shows drain slit well-closed with compact knot. Edges tightly approximated. Very short tails. No gap visible. Drain not avulsed. This is the best-confirmed final product quality since V15.

technique_summary: V30 at approximately 158 seconds represents a DRAMATIC RECOVERY from the V28-V29 equipment-change disaster (365s/295s), returning to the V22-V25 familiar-equipment plateau range. Critically, this is on a THIRD equipment setup — a blue FLS trainer with direct camera, similar to V15's setup but different from both the V25-V27 RCA-monitor station and the V28-V29 Vizio station. The fact that the trainee performed at 158s on yet another different setup (vs V28's 365s on its first attempt at a different station) suggests the V28-V29 catastrophe was suture-specific or instrument-specific, not a general equipment-transfer problem. The direct camera provides the HIGHEST CONFIDENCE score since V15 (0.62), confirming well-closed drain, short tails, no gap, and proper throw sequence. Cutting at 70s remains the bottleneck (44% of total time). The first throw at 30s is the longest phase — the surgeon's knot double wrap is inherently time-consuming but the subsequent single throws (14s, 18s) are efficient.
improvement_suggestions: RECOVERY CONFIRMED: 158s on new equipment vs V28's 365s on different new equipment. Equipment transfer is NOT universally impaired.
Cutting at 70s (44% of total time) remains the #1 bottleneck — consistent across all equipment configurations.
First throw at 30s is reasonable for a surgeon's knot but could potentially be faster with practice on this specific setup.
DIRECT CAMERA should be the STANDARD for all future recordings — dramatically improves scoring confidence (0.62 vs 0.30-0.45).
This setup (blue FLS trainer, direct camera) appears to work well. Consider using it as the primary practice station.
V28-V29 catastrophe was likely suture/instrument-specific. Investigate what was different about those specific instruments.
Placement at 25s is slightly slower than the 16-22s range on the RCA station — familiarization needed.
strengths: DRAMATIC RECOVERY from V28-V29 equipment disaster (365/295 → 158)
HIGHEST CONFIDENCE score since V15 (0.62) — direct camera provides excellent detail
CONFIRMED well-closed drain with no gap — first high-confidence drain assessment in 15 videos
Proper throw sequence confirmed: surgeon's knot → single → single with hand switches
Very short, clean tails — best-confirmed cutting quality
Drain not avulsed — 30 consecutive videos without avulsion
Equipment transfer skill demonstrated — performs well on third different station
Throws at 62s — reasonable, with fast throws 2+3 (14+18 = 32s)

phase_comparison.placement_25s: Slightly slow. V26: 16s. V27: 18s. V25: 22s. New equipment familiarization.
phase_comparison.cutting_70s: BOTTLENECK. V27: 53s. V26: 26s. V25: 67s. Reverted to slow cutting.

key_observations: 158s / 437 FLS — RECOVERY from V28-V29 disaster back to V22-V27 plateau range.
THIRD EQUIPMENT SETUP: Blue FLS trainer with direct camera. Different from both RCA and Vizio stations.
DIRECT CAMERA: Confidence 0.62 — highest since V15. Confirms drain well-closed, proper technique.
V28 (365s) was equipment-specific, NOT a general skill regression — proven by V30's 158s on another new setup.
Cutting 70s (44% of time) — the universal bottleneck regardless of equipment.
First throw 30s — surgeon's knot double wrap is time-consuming but correct technique.
Equipment adaptation: V28 station needed 365→295s to start adapting. V30 station: 158s on first attempt.
30th video. Three equipment configurations: RCA station (V11-V27, peak 94s), Vizio station (V28-V29, 365-295s), Blue trainer (V30, 158s).
